timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across Al Jazeera's Yunus interim government coverage (per user instruction). Cluster spec flags this subtopic as politically_sensitive: true; all entries are deliberately neutrally framed and event-anchored. Scoping note: c8_c focuses on Yunus's selection, appointment, governance decisions, reforms roadmap, controversies, and major events that occurred under his interim government. Differentiated from c8_a (protest mechanics), c8_b (Hasina personally and accountability arc), and c2_b (broad transition overview); the overlap on certain dates is unavoidable since Yunus's tenure provides the framing context for those events. Contributing articles from user-provided list: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/6/bangladesh-protesters-want-nobel-laureate-muhammad-yunus-to-lead-government ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/6/nobel-laureate-yunus-to-lead-bangladesh-interim-govt-presidents-office ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2024/8/6/bangladesh-protests-live-news-students-leaders-to-meet-army-chief (Aug 6 liveblog) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2024/8/7/big-challenges-ahead-for-muhammad-yunus-bangladeshs-new-interim-leader ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/7/nobel-winner-yunus-heads-home-as-bangladesh-prepares-for-interim-government ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/8/muhammad-yunus-returns-to-bangladesh-to-lead-interim-government ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/8/muhammad-yunus-takes-oath-as-head-of-bangladeshs-interim-government ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/17/bangladeshs-yunus-seeks-time-says-election-roadmap-after-reforms ; https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2025/5/24/why-is-bangladeshs-interim-leader-muhammad-yunus-considering-resigning ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/8/5/theyre-selling-the-revolution-bangladesh-a-year-after-hasinas-downfall ; https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2025/11/13/have-extrajudicial-killings-continued-under-bangladeshs-yunus-government ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/11/19/bangladeshs-test-after-hasina-conviction-will-it-repeat-her-mistakes . Additional contributing articles drawn from broader AJ coverage during Yunus's tenure to anchor key Yunus-era events: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/9/9/bangladesh-taking-steps-to-extradite-former-pm-hasina-from-india ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/18/bangladesh-ex-ministers-face-massacre-charges-hasina-probe-deadline-set ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/7/24/shoot-them-sheikh-hasina-ordered-firing-on-bangladesh-protesters-in-2024 ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/11/14/violence-in-bangladesh-as-toppled-hasinas-party-protests-crackdown-trial ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/12/19/bangladesh-on-edge-after-killing-of-2024-uprising-activist-triggers-unrest . Opinion piece read for context only (no entry drawn): https://www.aljazeera.com/opinions/2024/10/31/mohammad-yunus-can-go-down-in-history-as-the-architect-of-a-new-bangladesh . Article excluded as out of cluster window per rule 5: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/16/bangladeshs-interim-leader-yunus-steps-down-as-new-govt-set-to-take-over (Feb 16 2026 — postdates Dec 31 2025 cluster end). Articles from non-approved outlets excluded per project spec: https://www.ndtv.com/world-news/inside-muhammad-yunus-led-bangladesh-interim-government-who-are-its-key-members-6297421 (NDTV); https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/bangladesh-political-parties-reject-muhammad-yunuss-extended-time-frame-demand-election-by-december/articleshow/121583244.cms (Times of India); https://www.news18.com/world/world-sees-a-saint-we-saw-a-system-bangladeshs-ex-intel-officer-on-yunus-shadow-state-exclusive-ws-kl-9757993.html (News18). Date approximations per rule 4: Two same-date entries on Aug 6, Aug 7, and Aug 8 each preserved as distinct news developments per rule. Aug 7 entry (AJ challenges-ahead feature) pegged to publication date. May 24, 2025 entry pegged to AJ feature publication date — the underlying 'consideration of resignation' developed over weeks; May 24 used as the AJ-codified moment of the reporting. Aug 5, 2025 anniversary entry pegged to AJ piece publication date as the moment of the 'selling the revolution' critique framing. Nov 13, 19 entries pegged to AJ feature/analysis publication dates. Source disagreements: Yunus's tenure has been variously characterized — supporters credit reforms and accountability efforts, critics (especially within Awami League and from Indian observers) characterize the period as marked by extrajudicial activity, slow democratic restoration, and political imbalance. Entries report Yunus-era events factually without endorsing either characterization. The Nov 13, 2025 'extrajudicial killings continued' entry phrases AJ's framing as a question (which is how AJ's feature is titled) rather than an assertion. The 'reportedly considering resignation' framing for May 24 reflects AJ's reporting cadence; Yunus's office publicly denied an imminent resignation around that period. Tribunal proceedings against Hasina (Nov 17) reported with 'in absentia' noted; entries do not endorse the tribunal's process. Public figures named: Yunus, Hasina (as 'former prime minister'). No private individuals named. The Dec 19, 2025 activist whose killing triggered unrest is publicly identified in reporting but not named per rule 6 consistency.

Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across CNN's Bangladesh coverage of the Yunus interim government (per user instruction). CNN's Yunus-specific direct coverage is thinner than AJ's; the timeline therefore anchors on key Bangladesh events during Yunus's tenure that CNN covered, with framing that emphasizes Yunus's role/government for subtopic alignment. Contributing articles from user-provided list (in cluster window): https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/06/asia/bangladesh-protests-hasina-resignation-explainer-intl-hnk (covers Yunus naming) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/07/asia/muhammad-yunus-bangladesh-government-intl-hnk (the primary CNN Yunus piece) ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/02/05/asia/bangladesh-protest-hasina-house-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/02/12/asia/un-rights-office-bangladesh-protests-crackdown-intl-latam ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/05/13/asia/bangladesh-sheikh-hasina-awami-party-intl-hnk . Additional contributing articles drawn from broader CNN coverage during Yunus's tenure: https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/12/asia/bangladesh-quota-protests-student-martyr-intl-hnk-dst ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/14/asia/murder-probe-sheikh-hasina-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/09/03/asia/bangladesh-house-mirrors-detention-center-intl-hnk-dst ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/10/17/asia/bangladesh-arrest-warrant-sheikh-hasina-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/12/31/asia/bangladesh-march-hasina-student-protests-intl-hnk ; https://edition.cnn.com/2025/07/02/asia/bangladesh-pm-hasina-sentencing-jail-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/07/10/asia/bangladesh-pm-hasina-indicted-protesters-killed-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/11/17/asia/bangladesh-sheikh-hasina-verdict-intl-hnk ; https://edition.cnn.com/2025/12/01/asia/bangladesh-corruption-trial-tulip-siddiq-sheikh-hasina-intl-hnk . Article excluded as out of cluster window per rule 5: https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/09/asia/bangladesh-election-candidates-old-guard-intl-hnk (Feb 9 2026 — postdates Dec 31 2025 cluster end). Articles from non-approved outlets excluded per project spec: NDTV, Times of India, News18. Date approximations per rule 4: Aug 6 entry pegged to date of formal selection per CNN's Aug 6 explainer coverage; Aug 7 entry pegged to CNN's main Yunus profile piece publication. Aug 8 (sworn in) is the well-established date Yunus took the oath that evening. Sep 3 (Aynaghar) — pegged to CNN's Sep 3 piece publication; Yunus's actual inspection occurred days earlier but Sep 3 anchors the public revelation. Dec 16, 2024 (Yunus said elections could be held by end of 2025) — pegged to date of Yunus's underlying public statement, which CNN's Dec 31 piece referenced; per rule 4 the underlying event date is preferred over the reporting date. Jul 2, 2025 (Hasina sentenced) — phrased as 'imprisonment in absentia' generically; the specific charge basis is reported variously across sources and kept neutral here. The Tulip Siddiq Dec 1 2025 entry names her as she is a sitting UK Labour MP and a named defendant in formal corruption proceedings; permitted under rule 6 public figure exception. Source disagreements: CNN's framing of Yunus is more neutral-procedural than either pro-Yunus opinion pieces (AJ Oct 31 2024) or anti-Yunus critiques from Indian outlets (NDTV, News18, excluded). Entry framings reflect CNN's day-of reporting without endorsing either view of Yunus's tenure. The 'under Yunus's tenure' / 'under Yunus government' framings on many entries are subtopic-alignment devices rather than CNN's literal wording on each piece; CNN's coverage of these events typically did not use that exact framing but the events did factually occur under Yunus's government, which is the subtopic focus. Tribunal proceedings against Hasina noted as 'in absentia' for accuracy. Public figures named: Yunus, Hasina, Hasina's father Mujibur Rahman (deceased founding leader of Bangladesh), Tulip Siddiq (UK Labour MP, Hasina's niece, named defendant) — all public officials/figures of record. No private individuals named.
